text:
Once upon a time, in the heart of a lush, verdant forest, there was a small but mighty oak tree named Oakley. Oakley was not just any old oak; it was a tree with a special gift—the ability to communicate with all the creatures that lived around it. This gift made Oakley the guardian and protector of the forest.

One sunny morning, a tiny hummingbird named Hummie arrived at the edge of the forest, her wings tired and her feathers matted. She had flown for days without rest, seeking shelter from a terrible storm. As she fluttered around Oakley, her tiny body shivering, Oakley knew exactly what needed to be done.

"Come, little one," Oakley whispered softly, its branches swaying gently. "Climb up here, and I will share my strength with you."

Hummie perched on one of Oakley’s lowest branches, and as she did, a warm, comforting energy flowed from the tree into
